## 5 Most Influential Sources

### 1. web.dev - Font Best Practices (Google Chrome Team)
**URL:** https://web.dev/articles/font-best-practices
**One-sentence takeaway:** The canonical font performance guide from Google defines the complete font lifecycle (load, deliver, render), provides the `font-display` decision matrix with block/swap period tables, and is the authoritative reference for CWV-aware font optimization in 2026.
**Why critical for stinger-forge:** Every claim in `guides/00-principles.md` and `guides/06-performance-checklist.md` should trace back to this source.

### 2. James Ross Jr. - Font Loading Optimization (March 2026)
**URL:** https://www.jamesrossjr.com/blog/font-loading-optimization
**One-sentence takeaway:** The clearest explanation of FOIT/FOUT/FOFT differentiation plus the complete `size-adjust` + `ascent-override` metric matching pattern that eliminates CLS from font swapping in 2026.
**Why critical for stinger-forge:** The three-problem taxonomy (FOIT/FOUT/FOFT) and the fallback metric override CSS pattern are the core conceptual framework for `guides/00-principles.md`.

### 3. MDN - font-display descriptor
**URL:** https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@font-face/font-display
**One-sentence takeaway:** The specification anchor for `font-display` semantics, defining the three-period model (block, swap, failure) and confirming Baseline Widely Available status for all five values.
**Why critical for stinger-forge:** All font-display explanations in the stinger must anchor to this spec reference.

### 4. Modern CSS Tools - Fluid Typography with CSS clamp() (January 2026)
**URL:** https://moderncsstools.com/guides/fluid-typography/
**One-sentence takeaway:** The complete linear interpolation formula derivation for CSS `clamp()`, confirming 97%+ browser support and Baseline Widely Available status, plus the WCAG 1.4.4 accessibility requirement (mix vw with rem in the preferred value).
**Why critical for stinger-forge:** The slope/intercept formula derivation is the mathematical foundation of `guides/03-fluid-type-scale.md`.

### 5. Botmonster - Variable Font Subsetting (April 2026)
**URL:** https://botmonster.com/posts/variable-fonts-subsetting-web-performance/
**One-sentence takeaway:** The 2026 state-of-the-art for reducing variable font payload 90% (300 kB → 35 kB) using `pyftsubset`, with CI integration and the `size-adjust` metric override pattern to achieve zero CLS.
**Why critical for stinger-forge:** Provides the concrete byte budget targets (35 kB Latin subset) and the CI integration pattern that make performance claims actionable.

---

## 5 Open Questions for stinger-forge (or user) to resolve

### Q1: Does the project use `next/font`, Fontsource, or raw self-hosting?
This question is listed in the Command Brief and is the single most important determinant of the hosting strategy guide path. stinger-forge should structure `guides/01-hosting-strategy.md` as a decision tree with three branches: next/font (Next.js projects, recommended), Fontsource (non-Next.js React/Vite), raw self-hosting (maximum control, full subsetting pipeline).

### Q2: What is the canonical font-token naming convention for this project?
The research shows two common naming schemes: `--primitive-size-N` / `--text-body-size` (three-tier architecture) and `--step-N` (Utopia output). stinger-forge must decide which to adopt as the default in `templates/typography.css.template`, or provide both with guidance.

### Q3: Should the stinger default to `font-display: swap` or `font-display: optional` for body text?
The 2026 research consensus leans toward `optional` + preload for body text (best for CWV) while `swap` remains common (Google uses it in their own Fonts API). web.dev says "depends on priorities." stinger-forge should pick one as the opinionated default with clear rationale.

### Q4: Should the fluid type scale use `vw` or the newer `vi` viewport inline unit?
Utopia and some 2026 tools are transitioning to `vi` (viewport inline size), which is equivalent to `vw` for horizontal writing but correct for vertical writing modes. The research notes both but does not confirm universal Utopia adoption of `vi`. stinger-forge should check the current Utopia output and decide.

### Q5: How should the stinger handle the Fontsource manual @font-face optimization vs. default import?
Roy Portas's March 2026 article documents the optimization of dropping from ~12 `@font-face` rules to 3 by writing manual declarations. This is an advanced pattern. stinger-forge must decide whether to present this as the default Fontsource usage pattern or as an optional "advanced" section.

---

## Sources stinger-forge should re-fetch for deeper context

- **Smashing Magazine fluid type article (2022):** https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2022/01/modern-fluid-typography-css-clamp/ - Listed in Command Brief reference material. Evergreen content on the mathematical foundation of fluid type. The 2022 date is acceptable for spec-level content that hasn't changed.
- **Utopia.fyi main site:** https://utopia.fyi/ - The Command Brief references the Utopia calculator. The current output already fetched, but the companion philosophy articles may be worth reading for the "why" section of guides/03.
- **MDN Variable Fonts Guide:** https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_fonts/Variable_fonts_guide - The full guide (not just the @font-face syntax) for comprehensive variable font axis documentation.
- **Next.js Font API Reference:** https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/components/font - The full API reference beyond the getting-started docs, covering all options (display, preload, fallback, adjustFontFallback).
